content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

Print a permutation PPP of [1,2,…,N][1,2, \ldots, N][1,2,…,N] such that the following condition holds:


For any index i (1≤i<N)i\ (1 \leq i \lt N)i (1≤i<N),  Pi×Pi+1P_i \times P_{i+1}Pi​×Pi+1​ is not a perfect square.
If there are multiple correct answers, you may print **any** of them.

**Note:** A permutation of [1,2,…,N][1, 2, \ldots, N][1,2,…,N] is a rearrangement of those numbers.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the required permutation.


Output Format
For each test case, print NNN space-separated integers representing the valid permutation on a single line.


If there are multiple correct answers, you may print **any** of them.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: 2×1=22\times 1 = 22×1=2 is not a perfect square. Note that [1,2][1, 2][1,2] is also a valid answer for this case, and will be accepted.
Test case 222: 3×1=33\times 1 = 33×1=3 and 1×2=21\times 2 = 21×2=2 are not perfect squares.
Test case 333: 4×2=8,2×3=6,3×1=34\times 2 = 8, 2\times 3 = 6, 3\times 1 = 34×2=8,2×3=6,3×1=3 are all not perfect squares.
